zh.Brereton, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾布雷雷頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Philippa Elanor de Hulse於大約1415年出生在英國英格蘭爾麗頓 白 內斯頓時，她的父親Hugh Hulse55歲，母親Margery de Domville50歲。她和Sir William Brereton 10th Lord of Brereton至少有8個兒子和4個女兒。她去世於1463年11月29日在英國英格蘭爾布雷雷頓享年49歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hulse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): habitational name from Hulse in Witton (Cheshire), from Middle English holes, plural of hole ‘hole, hollow, depression’ (Old English holh). See Hole .
North German (Hülse): topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, Middle Low German huls, hüls.
American shortened and altered form of Flemish Holsaert (see Hulsart ). Compare Hulce , Huls , and Hults 1.

variants.name: Hulsart
meanings: Americanized form of Flemish Holsaert: unexplained. See also Hulse 3.

variants.name: Hole
meanings: English (southwestern): topographic name for someone who lived in or by a hole or hollow, from Middle English hol(e) (Old English hol, holh, dative hole), or a habitational name from some minor place so named.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads, so named from the dative singular or indefinite plural form of Old Norse hóll ‘round hill, mound’.

In some cases possibly also an American shortened form of Flemish Van Hole: habitational name from the common placename Hol(e), meaning ‘hollow, valley’; or a topographic name from the same term or from Middle Dutch hole, heule ‘(low) arched bridge, weir’.

variants.name: Hults
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin. Compare   Hultz   1.
In some cases also a variant of   Hultz   2, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Hulce
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin.

nameMeanings.name: Philippa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of Philip . In England during the Middle Ages the vernacular name Philip was borne by women as well as men, but female bearers were distinguished in Latin records by this form. It was not, however, used as a regular given name until the 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: Elanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Eleanor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

en.lifeSummary: When Philippa Elanor de Hulse was born about 1415, in Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Hugh Hulse, was 55 and her mother, Margery de Domville, was 50. She had at least 8 sons and 4 daughters with Sir William Brereton 10th Lord of Brereton. She died on 29 November 1463, in Brereton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49, and was buried in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.Leighton, Cheshire, England: Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Philippa Elanor de Hulse est née environ 1415, à Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh Hulse, avait 55 ans et sa mère, Margery de Domville, avait 50 ans. Elle a eu au moins 8 fils et 4 filles avec Sir William Brereton 10th Lord of Brereton. Elle est décédée le 29 novembre 1463, à Brereton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterrée à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.Leighton, Cheshire, England: Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Philippa Elanor de Hulse um 1415 in Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Hulse 55 und ihre Mutter Margery de Domville 50. Sie hatte mit Sir William Brereton 10th Lord of Brereton mindestens 8 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 29. November 1463 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Brereton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Morì il 29 novembre 1463, nel Brereton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolta a Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Philippa Elanor de Hulseが約1415年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアレイトン ビー ネストンで生まれた時，父Hugh Hulseは55歳，母Margery de Domvilleは50歳であった。彼女はSir William Brereton 10th Lord of Breretonとの間に少なくとも8人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1463年11月29日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアブレレトンで49歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスチェシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.Leighton, Cheshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 레이턴 비 네스턴
ko.lifeSummary: Philippa Elanor de Hulse(이)가 대략 1415년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 레이턴 비 네스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Hulse(은)는 55세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery de Domville(은)는 50세였다. 그녀는 Sir William Brereton 10th Lord of Brereton(와)과 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1463년 11월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 브레레턴에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Philippa Elanor de Hulse nasceu aproximadamente 1415, em Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Hugh Hulse, tinha 55 anos e sua mãe, Margery de Domville, tinha 50 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 8 filhos e 4 filhas com Sir William Brereton 10th Lord of Brereton. Ela faleceu em 29 de novembro de 1463, em Brereton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 49 anos, e foi sepultada em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Leighton, Cheshire, England: лиитон би нестон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Philippa Elanor de Hulse родилась около 1415 г. в местности лиитон би нестон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Hugh Hulse было 55 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery de Domville, 50. У неё было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 4 дочери от Sir William Brereton 10th Lord of Brereton. Она умерла 29 ноября 1463 г. в местности бриретон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет и была похоронена в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания.
